tools.name: bun
tools.label: bun
tools.description: Run TypeScript/JavaScript with Bun, manage packages, and run project scripts.
tools.promptSnippet: Run TypeScript or JavaScript, install packages, or execute package.json scripts with Bun.
promptGuidelines: Use 'bun run <script>' for package.json scripts (dev, build, bridge, diagnose, etc).
Use 'bun install' to add packages — do not use npm.
Use 'bun <file>.ts' to run TypeScript files directly without compilation.
Prefer bun over node/npm for all JS/TS operations in this project.

tools.name: rg
tools.label: rg
tools.description: Search file contents with ripgrep — fast regex search.
tools.promptSnippet: Search file contents with ripgrep using explicit argv arguments.
promptGuidelines: Pass search pattern and flags as separate argv items, e.g. ["-n", "pattern", "path"].
Use --glob for file filtering instead of shell globs.
Use this tool instead of bash for code search and grep tasks.

tools.name: fd
tools.label: fd
tools.description: Find files and directories by name or pattern.
tools.promptSnippet: Find files and directories by name or pattern.
promptGuidelines: Pass pattern and path as separate argv items.
Use -e for extension filtering, -t for type filtering.
Use this tool instead of bash find for file discovery.

tools.name: jq
tools.label: jq
tools.description: Query and transform JSON with jq.
tools.promptSnippet: Query and transform JSON data with jq filters.
promptGuidelines: Pass the jq filter and flags as separate argv items.
Use this tool instead of bash for JSON processing.

tools.name: bat
tools.label: bat
tools.description: Display file contents with syntax highlighting.
tools.promptSnippet: Display file contents with syntax highlighting via bat.
promptGuidelines: Use --style=plain for scripts and piping.
Use --line-range for viewing specific sections.
Use --language to force syntax highlighting for a specific language.

tools.name: tree
tools.label: tree
tools.promptSnippet: Show a directory tree with tree.
promptGuidelines: Use -L to limit depth.
Use -I to ignore patterns.

tools.name: eza
tools.label: eza
tools.description: List directory contents with colors and icons.
tools.promptSnippet: List directory contents with eza (modern ls).
promptGuidelines: Use -la for detailed list with hidden files.
Use -T for tree view.
Use --sort=modified to list by modification time.
